title: zygogynum ledermannii
intro: Zygogynum ledermannii, also known as the "Queensland rosewood", is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Winteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves and bark of the Zygogynum ledermannii plant are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and skin infections, offering health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In traditional herbal medicine, preparations of the plant are made by infusing its leaves and bark in hot water or oil, and are used to create remedies in the form of teas, ointments, and tonics.
